name: Push/Pull/Legs Split
description: A 3-day Push/Pull/Legs split built from @KarlApexFit's 65-exercise database covering every muscle group. Run 3x/week or 6x/week (PPL x2).
creator: @KarlApexFit
tips: Run as 3x/week (Push, Pull, Legs) or 6x/week (PPL × 2) depending on recovery and schedule
Each workout covers one movement pattern thoroughly
Exercises drawn from a curated 65-exercise database covering every muscle group
restGuidelines: 2-3 min rest between compound working sets. 60-90s between isolation sets.

overview.goal: Build strength and size with a clean Push/Pull/Legs split. Simple, proven structure that covers every muscle group across 3 sessions.
overview.whoIsItFor: Beginner to intermediate lifters who want a straightforward gym program. Works great for people with 3 or 6 days to train — run the cycle once or twice per week.
overview.whatToExpect: Three focused workouts: Push (chest, shoulders, triceps), Pull (back, biceps, rear delts), and Legs (quads, hamstrings, glutes, calves). Each session covers one movement pattern thoroughly using compound and isolation work.
keyPrinciples: Push/Pull/Legs is the most popular split for a reason — simple and balanced
Run 3x/week for a sustainable schedule or 6x/week for maximum frequency
Compound movements first, isolation work after
65 exercises curated to cover every muscle group comprehensively
Add weight or reps each week to ensure progressive overload
overview.cycleNote: This is a repeating cycle — run Push/Pull/Legs on repeat. 3x/week means one cycle per week, 6x/week means two cycles per week. Progress by adding weight or reps.
overview.weeklyStructure: 3x: Push → Pull → Legs (+ rest days) | 6x: Push → Pull → Legs → Push → Pull → Legs → Rest
